name: agenda

capacity.note: past six sections the list crowds

density.note: a short agenda line, not a description

description: Auto-numbered table of contents for the deck.
purpose: Use as the second slide of any multi-section deck. Numbers are generated; authors just write the section titles. Five interchangeable styles — the default `ledger` (a contents page with optional page references), plus `circles`, `rail`, `cards`, and `checks` — all compose with the `progress-N` 'you are here' modifier.

title.description: Slide heading — typically 'Agenda' or 'What we'll cover'.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: agenda -->

## What this deck covers.

1. First section title
2. Second section title
3. Third section title
4. Fourth section title
sample:
<!-- _class: agenda -->

## What this review covers.

1. Where Q2 landed against plan `p.3`
2. The enterprise renewal shortfall `p.8`
3. What we are changing in Q3 `p.14`
4. The resourcing ask `p.21`
5. Risks we are watching `p.27`
stressSample:
<!-- _class: agenda progress-4 -->

## Where we are now.

1. Why we're here — the problem we set out to solve `p.3`
2. Where the business landed against plan `p.9`
3. The proposal on the table `p.16`
4. Migration plan and sequencing `p.24`
5. Risks and the mitigations we're funding `p.31`
6. The decision and immediate next steps `p.38`

whenToUse.title: Second slide of the deck
whenToUse.body: Right after the title, before the first section. Orients the audience and sets the cadence of what's coming.

whenToUse.title: Three to six sections
whenToUse.body: Sweet spot is four. Past six sections the list crowds and the audience stops counting. Roll up or split the deck.

whenToUse.title: Reuse with progress variants
whenToUse.body: Drop the same agenda between sections with `progress-N` to show how far through the deck the room is. Lightweight wayfinding.

whenToUse.title: Pick a style, optionally with page refs
whenToUse.body: The default `ledger` reads as a contents page; add `circles`, `rail`, `cards`, or `checks` to change the structure. On `ledger`, end an item with an inline-code page ref — e.g. `` `p.15` `` — to print a right-aligned page number with a leader; omit it for just number + title.

antiPatterns.title: Sub-bullets per section
antiPatterns.body: The agenda is a wayfinder, not a treatment. If a section needs decomposition, that belongs on a section divider when the section opens — not here.

antiPatterns.title: Unnumbered list
antiPatterns.body: Authoring with `-` instead of `1.` loses the numbered chrome the layout depends on. Always use ordered list syntax.

antiPatterns.title: Single-section decks
antiPatterns.body: If the deck has no sections to enumerate, skip the agenda. Empty wayfinding is more friction than no wayfinding.

antiPatterns.title: More than six sections
antiPatterns.body: A single agenda slide holds up to six sections at a legible row height; beyond that the rows crowd the footer. Group related items under fewer headings, or split the agenda across two slides.

related.name: divider
related.when: marking a section boundary without restating the menu

related.name: title
related.when: the slide immediately preceding the agenda

progress-2.label: Progress · section 2
progress-2.caption: The same agenda dropped between sections one and two — the second item is marked as the current position.
progress-2.sample:
<!-- _class: agenda progress-2 -->

## Where we are now.

1. The four-layer model — Function · Form · Substance · Finish
2. Component manifests — the single source of truth
3. The forty-five shipped components, grouped by function
4. Discovery — scaffolder, snippets, this gallery
5. What ships next — open questions and follow-ups

progress-3.label: Progress · section 3
progress-3.caption: Same wayfinding pattern, current position moved to the third item.

circles.label: Style · circles
circles.caption: Each section number sits in a drawn ring; with `progress-N` the current ring fills with the accent. A calmer, more graphic take on the contents page.
circles.sample:
<!-- _class: agenda circles progress-3 -->

## Where we are now.

1. The four-layer model
2. Component manifests
3. The shipped components
4. Discovery — scaffolder & snippets
5. What ships next

rail.label: Style · rail
rail.caption: Numbered nodes threaded on a vertical journey line — reads as moving down the deck. The active node fills with the accent.
rail.sample:
<!-- _class: agenda rail progress-3 -->

## Where we are now.

1. The four-layer model
2. Component manifests
3. The shipped components
4. Discovery — scaffolder & snippets
5. What ships next

cards.label: Style · cards
cards.caption: Each section is a boxed row; the current card fills with the accent wash and takes the accent border.
cards.sample:
<!-- _class: agenda cards progress-3 -->

## Where we are now.

1. The four-layer model
2. Component manifests
3. The shipped components
4. Discovery — scaffolder & snippets
5. What ships next

checks.label: Style · checks
checks.caption: A progress checklist: with `progress-N`, sections already covered get a tick, the current one an arrow, and upcoming ones an empty box — the whole journey-state at a glance.
checks.sample:
<!-- _class: agenda checks progress-3 -->

## Where we are now.

1. The four-layer model
2. Component manifests
3. The shipped components
4. Discovery — scaffolder & snippets
5. What ships next
